description: The golden set contains only easy representative cases, so a high average score hides failures on hard and adversarial inputs.
body: Failure mode: the eval set is built from typical, easy examples, so the system scores high on average while failing the rare-but-critical and adversarial cases that are never measured. Detection signals: no hard/adversarial/edge segments; a single blended average; production failures on cases absent from the set. Do not report one average over an easy-only set. Safer fallback: deliberately include known failure modes, adversarial inputs, and edge cases, segment scores by case type so weak categories surface, and require failure-case coverage before trusting an aggregate score.
